stem: When the quadratic function f is graphed in the xy-plane, where y = f(x), its vertex is (5, −12). One of the x-intercepts of this graph is (−3, 0). What is the other x-intercept of the graph?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. For a parabola, the x-coordinate of the vertex is the midpoint of the two x-intercepts. Let the other x-intercept be x = q. Then (−3 + q)/2 = 5, so q = 13. The other x-intercept is (13, 0).

Choice A and Choice B fail the midpoint equation. Choice D has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
